purpose: Compatibility contract for the Node/TypeScript runtime.

scenarios.description: No managed store yet; bare command should start first-run mode and scans should be empty.
Inbox contains unmanaged skills ready for import; import dry-run writes nothing and import apply does not enable.
External skill can be adopted, enabled in desired state, diffed, and materialized.
Existing Claude/Codex rendered skills include identical merge candidates and same-name different-content fork candidates.
Global/project/session manifests exercise disable-mask precedence and client-specific enable/disable buckets.
Rendered target has an unmanaged real directory that materialize and rollback must not overwrite/remove.
Skill-looking location has a broken symlink and must be diagnosed instead of blindly removed.
Candidate directories missing SKILL.md are reported and cannot be adopted as valid skills.
Materialize symlink failure falls back to a manager-marked copy that rollback can safely remove.
Partial materialization journal remains doctor/rollback-compatible across runtimes.
Backup includes canonical managed state and rendered-output metadata only; pre-migration backup raw-copies rendered dirs and inbox; restore does not materialize.
Preset create/show/list/add/remove/rename/delete/apply flows mutate only preset JSON or manifests as documented.
Preset entries whose aliases drift from managed metadata remain inspectable and doctorable.
Preset apply with unknown managed IDs fails atomically before manifest mutation.
Malformed preset files are reported by doctor without preventing unrelated preset inspection.
Dry-runs and previews never append logs; applied mutations append safe metadata only.
